hard.next_step: Justin to send Zycus' RFP template.
hard.ais_why:
• Touro University's meeting showed 'positive reception to AI contract agent and AP Smartdesk features,' with interest from Maribel Giraldo 'in reducing rogue spend' and highlighted 'Autonomous Contract Agent' capability.
• Merlin Intake is scoped and the Agentic-platform-discussed flag is set to Yes, confirming AI features were engaged with positively.
• The compelling event is a Unimarket renewal roughly a year out, so the positive AI reception is not yet backed by near-term urgency, keeping it curious.

north_star_verdict.headline: One discovery call, then quiet. Maribel Giraldo opened by saying she is 'not looking to rip or replace, just trying to learn about the competition' (16 Jun); her reference and pricing asks are still unanswered, no economic buyer is mapped, and Unimarket's renewal is roughly a year out. This is a market scan, not an active evaluation.
north_star_verdict.math: Deal created 2026-06-17, 19 days old, in Qualified stage since 2026-06-18. Close date 2027-02-18 is ~227 days out. Time-in-stage (~18 days) is normal for early discovery against a 12-15 month enterprise cycle, and no forward slip has occurred. But only one buyer call exists (2026-06-16), the next step is a rep-side 'send the RFP template', and the last activity (2026-06-16) is now ~20 days old with no buyer follow-up. With no compelling event, no economic buyer, and a buyer in explicit market-scan mode, the pace needed to reach Vendor Selected in the next two quarters is not present, so momentum is thinning, not dead.
evidence: Maribel Giraldo opened the 2026-06-16 call: 'we use Unimarket as our eProcurement platform. It's been here since I've been here... I'm not looking to make any changes, rip or replace. This is just me trying to learn a little bit about the competition.' The posture is benchmarking, not buying.

Maribel stated her reference requirement as a blocker: 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education' (2026-06-16). Only Western Governors University was offered as proof.

Maribel asked twice for pricing: 'I would be interested in looking at your price structure' and 'would you be able to share pricing even if it's just superficial pricing per module?' (2026-06-16). Neither was delivered; Next_Step__c is 'Justin to send Zycus' RFP template', which does not mention pricing.
No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or executive sponsor is identified. Maribel is Director of Procurement Services (Evaluator contact role), not a confirmed budget authority for a platform decision.

Unimarket has been deployed 8-9 years and 'serves a purpose'; the contract module is owned but unused ('We've done demos for the contract module, but no traction yet'). Qualitative pain only, no quantified case, no compelling event.

Seven Opportunity-Health tasks on 2026-06-18 (Call Attempted) flag 'Prospect's gone cold, no inbound since last 14 days', 'Decision Maker is not identified', and 'Keep the engagement multi-threaded' -- corroborating a single-threaded, cooling early deal.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. On the 2026-06-16 discovery call Maribel Giraldo described qualitative pain: 'We are just very big and still operating in silos' across Touro's multi-state campuses, and the Unimarket contract module is owned but unused ('We've done demos for the contract module, but no traction yet'). She acknowledged rogue spend exists ('Of course. Everywhere, I suppose, where there are people'). But she attached no dollars, hours, headcount, or leakage figures to any of it, and Justin did not probe for metrics. Without a costed pain there is no fuel for an executive business case. The move is to run a light value-scoping with Maribel, anchored to a higher-ed peer such as Western Governors University, to put a number on siloed procurement and rogue spend.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. The only mapped contact is Maribel Giraldo, Director of Procurement Services (Evaluator, primary contact role). For a modular Merlin Intake add-on (~$50k) she may control the procurement budget, and she drove the commercial questions herself ('I would be interested in looking at your price structure', 2026-06-16), which is a partial signal that she at least influences the spend. But for a broader platform decision a Touro finance or operations leader would need to sponsor it, and none has been named on any call or in any field. The 2026-06-18 Opportunity-Health task 'Decision Maker is not identified' corroborates the gap. The move is to ask Maribel to introduce whoever owns the procurement technology budget across the campuses.
sources: SF Task 2026-06-18 'Decision Maker is not identified'

decision_criteria.narrative: One hard criterion is confirmed and explicit: proven higher-education representation. Maribel said 'that's actually one of my requirements... I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education' (2026-06-16). A second implied criterion is non-disruption -- she inherited Unimarket, is 'not looking to rip or replace', and reacted with interest when Justin positioned Merlin Intake as deployable 'on top of existing systems such as Unimarket' ('Okay. That's interesting'). Price transparency is a third: she asked twice for per-module pricing. A full evaluation rubric (weighted requirements, an RFP scorecard) has not been shared and likely does not exist yet, which is consistent with a market-scan stage.

decision_process.narrative: There is no visible decision process. Only one Zycus-Touro call has happened (2026-06-16), attended solely by Maribel Giraldo on the buyer side, so the call sequence reveals no committee, no stage gates, and no approval chain. Maribel framed the engagement as personal market research ('just me trying to learn a little bit about the competition') rather than a sanctioned evaluation with a timeline. No RFP has been issued (the RFP template is something Zycus offered to provide), and no budget-approval or procurement-process detail was discussed. The move is to establish whether Touro will run a formal evaluation and, if so, who sits on it and by when.
sources: SF field history (single stage change Initial Interest -> Qualified 2026-06-18)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown and appropriate to note as a gap this early. No MSA, order form, SOW, legal, procurement channel, or InfoSec discussion occurred on the 2026-06-16 call, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated. The one forcing function on the horizon is the incumbent Unimarket renewal, referenced in the AIS rationale as 'roughly a year out' -- if dated precisely it becomes the natural contracting clock. What would create visibility is confirming the Unimarket renewal/notice date and Touro's procurement-approval path for a new software vendor.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is real but low-intensity and not senior-owned. Maribel Giraldo (Director of Procurement Services) named three operational pains on 2026-06-16: multi-campus silos ('We are just very big and still operating in silos'), an unused Unimarket contract module ('We've done demos for the contract module, but no traction yet'), and rogue spend she conceded exists 'everywhere... where there are people'. Merlin Intake's rogue-spend control and the autonomous contract agent landed as relevant. But she repeatedly softened the pain -- Unimarket 'serves a purpose', she is 'not looking to rip or replace' -- so this is discomfort, not a crisis, and it is owned by a procurement director, not a CFO or provost who would fund a replacement. The move is to quantify the silo/rogue-spend cost with her and find the senior leader who feels it.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-06-16 (Maribel Giraldo, pain owner)

champion.narrative: There is no champion yet -- Maribel Giraldo is at best an engaged coach. She hosted the 2026-06-16 discovery call, asked detailed questions about Merlin Intake, contract management, and pricing, and requested follow-up materials, so she is a genuine point of contact. But she is not advocating for Zycus: she opened by saying she is not looking to replace Unimarket, she surfaced a barrier rather than working to overcome it ('very little representation within higher education'), she has introduced no other stakeholder, shared no internal document, and named no timeline. Her contact role is Evaluator, which matches her behaviour -- she is assessing whether Zycus clears her bar, not selling Zycus internally. To develop her, deliver her two asks (higher-ed references and pricing) and earn an introduction to a budget owner.

competition.narrative: The primary competitor is the status quo: Unimarket, the incumbent Maribel inherited, deployed 8-9 years across every campus in one instance, which 'serves a purpose' with no active replacement mandate. That makes do-nothing the most likely outcome, not a loss to another platform. The Salesforce Competitors__c field lists Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and JAGGAER, but none were raised as active alternatives on the 2026-06-16 call; Maribel only name-dropped the 'big players' generically ('Koopa, Jagger, Unimarket, I think Workday now') while admitting 'I've never heard of Zycus'. So the real fight is inertia plus a reference objection, not a live bake-off. The wedge for Zycus is Merlin Intake as a non-disruptive AI overlay that runs on top of Unimarket, removing the rip-and-replace risk, paired with named higher-ed references to neutralize the representation concern.

deal_movement.summary: New deal, one buyer touch, now quiet. Created 2026-06-17, discovery call 2026-06-16, advanced Initial Interest -> Qualified on 2026-06-18, reassigned to Justin Ajmo on 2026-06-17 and re-divisioned to Mid-Enterprise on 2026-07-03. The only buyer-facing event is the single 2026-06-16 call; the next step (RFP template) is rep-side and pending, and no buyer follow-up has landed in ~20 days.

items.change: Discovery call held with Maribel Giraldo; Zycus overview + Merlin Intake / autonomous contract agent walkthrough via screenshots (no live demo available)
Seven Opportunity-Health call-attempt tasks logged flagging cold prospect, unidentified decision maker, and single-threading

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is status-quo inertia: Unimarket is an 8-9 year incumbent that 'serves a purpose', Maribel is explicitly 'not looking to rip or replace', and there is no compelling event, so do-nothing is the likely default. Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and JAGGAER are logged in Salesforce Competitors__c but were not raised as active rivals on the 2026-06-16 call, so they are dormant, not live. Workday was only name-dropped in passing. No competitor is buyer-preferred because no active evaluation is running. We win by landing Merlin Intake as a no-rip overlay on Unimarket and answering the higher-ed reference objection.

competitors.name: Status Quo / Do Nothing
competitors.quote: I'm not looking to make any changes, rip or replace. This is just me trying to learn a little bit about the competition.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin Intake as an add-on that runs on top of Unimarket, a beachhead that needs no rip-and-replace, plus a quantified rogue-spend / silo cost to create urgency ahead of the Unimarket renewal.

competitors.name: Unimarket
competitors.quote: we use Unimarket as our eProcurement platform. It's been here since I've been here, so I inherited... it serves a purpose.
competitors.how_we_win: Coexist first (Merlin Intake overlay), then expose Unimarket's sourcing-flexibility and CLM-adoption gaps ('we don't use it') to grow into contract management and sourcing.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Listed in SF Competitors__c; on the call only name-dropped generically ('Koopa'), not evaluated.
competitors.how_we_win: If Coupa becomes active, contrast Zycus organically-built single-code-base suite and a-la-carte modular pricing against Coupa's cost.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Listed in SF Competitors__c; on the call only name-dropped generically ('Jagger'), not evaluated.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI agents (intake, autonomous contract agent, risk radar) as the differentiated workflow-automation story.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Listed in SF Competitors__c; not mentioned on the 2026-06-16 call.
competitors.how_we_win: No active signal; monitor. Differentiate on AI depth and higher-ed references if GEP surfaces in a formal RFP.

competitors.name: Workday
competitors.quote: I've heard of the big players, Koopa, Jagger, Unimarket, I think Workday now. Right?
competitors.how_we_win: Only a passing mention; if it surfaces, contrast procurement-native AI depth against Workday's ERP-adjacent breadth.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Two expectations are clear and one is a live exposure. Maribel needs proven higher-education representation (currently exposed, only WGU offered) and a non-disruptive path that does not force replacing Unimarket (well-aligned via the Merlin Intake overlay). Price transparency is requested and not yet met.

items.quote: Only Maribel Giraldo (Evaluator) is engaged; no budget authority for a platform decision named on any call or field.
Unimarket renewal referenced as 'roughly a year out' but no dated forcing function or decision timeline stated.
We are just very big and still operating in silos -- pain described, never costed.
Buyer framed the engagement as personal market research; no committee, RFP, or approval path defined.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, single-threaded, status-quo-anchored deal. The three highest-leverage win levers are neutralizing the reference objection, landing a no-rip beachhead, and multi-threading to a budget owner.
flags: Neutralize the reference objection now: lead with named multi-campus higher-ed customers plus Western Governors University's realized ROI before Maribel's hesitation hardens.
Land small: position Merlin Intake as a no-rip AI overlay on Unimarket to win a beachhead without a rip-and-replace fight, then grow into unused contract management and sourcing.
Multi-thread past a single Evaluator: earn an introduction to the finance or operations leader who owns the procurement-technology budget across the campuses.

items.name: Maribel Giraldo
items.title: Director of Procurement Services
items.risk: Engaged evaluator in market-scan mode, not advocating for Zycus. Surfaced a higher-ed reference objection and asked for pricing; both unanswered. Has introduced no other stakeholder. Will cool without her two asks delivered and a reason to move.

champion_strength.summary: No true champion. Maribel Giraldo is an engaged evaluator/coach who hosted the 2026-06-16 discovery call and asked substantive questions, but she opened by ruling out a rip-and-replace, surfaced a reference barrier rather than fighting it, and has introduced no one else. She helps Justin understand Touro's concerns; she is not selling Zycus internally. She is at risk of cooling unless her references and pricing asks are met quickly.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The AI story landed cleanly: Maribel reacted positively to Merlin Intake, the autonomous contract agent, and the rogue-spend control angle, and was intrigued that Merlin Intake can deploy on top of Unimarket. AI is Zycus's clearest wedge here, but it is not yet backed by urgency or a quantified case.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate matches the call: genuine curiosity about Merlin Intake, the autonomous contract agent, and rogue-spend reduction, but no urgency and no quantified AI value yet. AI is the strongest reason this deal exists, so it is fairly rated, not under-rated.

items.detail: Status-quo inertia is the core risk: an 8-9 year Unimarket incumbency that 'serves a purpose', buyer explicitly not seeking to replace, no compelling event -- do-nothing is the likely outcome.
Higher-education reference gap is an explicit objection; only Western Governors University offered as proof.
No economic buyer or decision maker mapped; single-threaded on one Evaluator.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: One discovery call with a procurement director who is market-scanning, not replacing: Unimarket is an entrenched incumbent, the only forcing event (a Unimarket renewal) is roughly a year out, and Maribel's reference and pricing asks are still unanswered.

win_position.text: Early, low-conviction deal: Maribel Giraldo (Director of Procurement Services) opened the 16 Jun call saying she is 'not looking to make any changes, rip or replace, just trying to learn about the competition', so Zycus is being benchmarked, not selected.

Her explicit requirement is a live blocker: 'I would be very hesitant if we were to switch out of Unimarket to go with a company that has very little representation within higher education' (16 Jun), and only Western Governors University has been offered as proof.
No economic buyer is mapped and there is no compelling event; the Unimarket renewal is roughly a year out, so nothing yet forces a decision.

deal_momentum.text: Only one buyer call has occurred (16 Jun); the next step is a rep-side 'send the RFP template', no buyer follow-up has landed in ~20 days, and a 18 Jun health task flags the prospect as gone cold.
Engagement was a screenshot walkthrough, not a live demo or POC: Maribel asked 'would you be able to show me how it looks in real life' and Justin could not demo.
Commercial topics did enter early: Maribel asked twice to see the per-module price structure (16 Jun), a mild buying signal inside an otherwise research posture.

customer_commitment.text: Maribel is an Evaluator running personal market research, not a sponsor; she introduced no other stakeholder and called Unimarket a system that 'serves a purpose' (16 Jun).
No quantified pain: silos and unused CLM were described ('we are just very big and still operating in silos') but never costed (16 Jun).
Genuine module-level interest: she named contract management (owned but unused), sourcing, and Merlin Intake as an add-on on top of Unimarket (16 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Primary threat is status-quo inertia: an 8-9 year Unimarket incumbency with no compelling event, so a no-decision is the likely outcome rather than a loss to a rival (16 Jun).
Single-threaded on one Evaluator with no economic buyer or decision maker identified (18 Jun health task: 'Decision Maker is not identified').
The higher-education reference gap is an unresolved objection that could end the evaluation before it formally starts (16 Jun).

generic_demo_only.evidence: Screenshots only, no live demo; buyer in market-scan mode (16 Jun).

champion.evidence: Maribel is a weak evaluator/coach, not advocating; no internal introductions.

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB or DM mapped; single-threaded (18 Jun health task).

compelling_event.evidence: Only a Unimarket renewal ~1 year out; no dated forcing function.

customer_preference.evidence: 'Not looking to rip or replace, just learning about the competition' (16 Jun).

scope_change.detail: The deal was created at this scope; no narrowing versus a prior record. Merlin Intake as a no-rip overlay is deliberately a small landing motion, with the full suite (sourcing, CLM, eProcurement, eInvoicing, spend analysis, supplier risk) discussed only as future expansion.

expansion_context.note: No prior Closed-Won deal on Touro University; this is a genuine new-business early-stage deal, so the unmapped economic buyer and single-thread ARE real risks (no inherited executive access).

commentary.win_position: Win 0 reflects a weak position: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 1 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
